title: Dead Code, Unused Dependencies, and Reachability · Policy Workshop
summary: Remove unreachable code and dependencies with evidence rather than guesswork. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a dead-code and dependency audit while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Treat unused imports, variables, parameters, private members, and exports according to language semantics.
Detect unreachable branches, impossible states, and obsolete feature paths.
Detect unused, missing, duplicate, and undeclared dependencies.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append a gates-safe-removal-1256aw: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Safe Removal into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-safe-removal-1256aw:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Safe Removal condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Safe Removal applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Remove dead code with tests, telemetry, deprecation, and rollback evidence proportionate to risk.
